commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the iMessage sender so an explicitly disabled `channels.imessage.actions.reply` prevents `replyToId` from being turned into bridge/RPC reply parameters.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main turns any supplied `replyToId` into iMessage `reply_to` without checking `actions.reply`. A live macOS SIP reproduction was not provided in this PR.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor/maintainer proof and test coverage, not a safe ClawSweeper repair lane because automation cannot prove the contributor's real macOS setup.

target.title: fix(imessage): respect actions.reply=false in outbound delivery
target.body:
## Summary

When `channels.imessage.actions.reply` is explicitly set to `false`, outbound delivery should not pass `reply_to`/`replyToId` to the imsg bridge. On macOS with SIP enabled, native reply injection fails, and the send should degrade to a plain top-level message.

## Fix

In `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`, the `resolvedReplyToId` now checks `account.config.actions?.reply !== false` before resolving the replyToId:

```diff
- const resolvedReplyToId = sanitizeReplyToId(opts.replyToId);
+ const resolvedReplyToId =
+   account.config.actions?.reply !== false ? sanitizeReplyToId(opts.replyToId) : undefined;
```

## Files changed

- `extensions/imessage/src/send.ts`: +2/-1 lines

## Verification

- [x] Existing iMessage send tests pass
- [x] When `actions.reply === false`, replyToId is `undefined` and no `reply_to` param is passed to the bridge

## Related

Fixes #92142
